title: RSS 2.0, Atom 1.0, Namespaces, and Extension Policy · Contract Triage
summary: A preserved Go feed-service run contains one plausible discovery, URL, XML, normalization, fetch, SQL, transaction, lease, goroutine, handler, accessibility, migration, or release defect; locate the first failed invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in standards-aware syndication vocabulary map and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and e…
objectives: Map RSS channel and item required, optional, repeated, GUID, enclosure, category, source, date, and extension constructs.
Map Atom feed and entry identifiers, authors, links, text constructs, content, categories, dates, xml:base, and xml:lang.
Compare XML expanded names rather than prefixes and preserve unknown extension provenance without inventing semantics.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Feed Go Rss Channel Item
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug Go team handles case 6926 during a cross-tenant privacy audit. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove RSS and Atom format semantics, expanded names, variance, and extension restraint; failed, canceled, stale, and cross-tenant operations must preserve the prior admitted state; then inject one URL, DNS, redirect, compression, entity, depth, timeout, or publisher-budget failure and reject it safely. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Map RSS channel and item required, optional, repeated, GUID, enclosure, category, source, date, and extension constructs.

Inspect the Feed Go Rss Channel Item trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Feed Go Extension Policy
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: feed-go-extension-policy-aygn50" that uses case 7103 to prove RSS and Atom format semantics, expanded names, variance, and extension restraint. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, parse through native XML libraries, contact publishers, access PostgreSQL, read host files, handle real signals, launch unbounded goroutines, or claim race, load, accessibility, container, backup, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5, pgx 5.10.0, goose 3.27.2, PostgreSQL 18.4, original fixtures, controlled RoundTrippers and httptest servers, disposable databases, race and fuzz runs, subprocesses, inspected images, accessibility review, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, and release gates.

content.text: A debug Go team handles case 1596 during a publisher-fairness incident rehearsal. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove RSS and Atom format semantics, expanded names, variance, and extension restraint; every goroutine, body, row set, transaction, lease, timer, and pool needs named cleanup ownership; then change one tenant, cursor, reader-state, authorization, cache, or accessibility condition without leaking state. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Allowlist interpreted extensions, bound ignored markup, and surface unsupported constructs for review.
